gold.rationale: The article mixes real social-media reactions around #UFC328 with a misleading ratings narrative built from an edited screenshot. Its main problem is that unverified AI-chat material is reframed as credible overnight audience evidence and visually packaged to look like legitimate broadcast-ratings reporting.

sentence_level.sentence_text: UFC's official account called it a wild night of fights and finishes, and the Newark show quickly became one of the most talked-about topics in sports online after Sean Strickland edged Khamzat Chimaev for the middleweight title and Joshua Van stopped Tatsuro Taira in the fifth round of the co-main event.

Joshua Van stopped Tatsuro Taira in the fifth round of the co-main event.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Early screenshots shared after the event pointed to a strong CBS audience number, and posts online were already casting UFC 328 as a breakout network showing for the promotion.

paragraph_level: UFC 328 was a chaotic and action-packed event that kept fans engaged from the prelims to the main event.
The event was described by UFC's official account as a wild night of fights and finishes.
Sean Strickland edged Khamzat Chimaev for the middleweight title at UFC 328.
Joshua Van stopped Tatsuro Taira in the fifth round of the co-main event.
The reaction to the event moved almost as fast as the fights themselves.
Natalie Loureda provided fighter-adjacent reaction referencing Joshua Van's country colors.
Fans traded clips, debated scores, and called for future actions across the timeline.
The hashtag #UFC328 generated live buzz comparable to cards with decisive moments.
Early screenshots indicated a strong CBS audience number for the event.
Posts online cast UFC 328 as a breakout network showing for the promotion.
A polished ratings graphic circulated in fight circles, strengthening the view of the event's connection between arena and broadcast.
The takeaway from social media is that UFC caught fire on fight night and may be riding that momentum into its next event.

visual_indicators.scene_description: A data infographic detailing the ratings for UFC 328. It features a large header, a highlighted box with average viewership numbers (3.1 million average, 4.2 million peak), and bullet points on demographic performance and comparisons to previous broadcasts.
visual_indicators.ocr_text: UFC 328 OVERNIGHT AUDIENCE CHATTER Reports on the ratings for UFC 328, which aired on CBS on Saturday, May 9, 2026, indicate that the event was a significant viewership success for both the network and TKO Group Holdings. According to preliminary data and industry analysis: Average Viewership: The broadcast averaged 3.1 million viewers, peaking at 4.2 million during the main event title fight. Demographic Performance: The event dominated the Saturday night primetime slot in the key MEN 18-49 and MEN 18-34 demographics, marking CBS's best performance in those categories for a Saturday night in the 2025-2026 television season. Comparison: This event outperformed the last major network broadcast (UFC on ABC in late 2025) by approximately 15%, benefiting from the strong lead-in of regional sports programming and a high-stakes championship headliner. UFC 328 CBS Ratings: Strong Numbers for Newark Return (MMA Fighting). TV Ratings: UFC 328 Delivers for CBS on Saturday Night (Programming Insider). Viewership Data: UFC 328 Main Event Peaks Over 4 Million (Sports Business Journal).
visual_indicators.image_text_alignment_hint: The image explicitly states the date as May 9, 2026, which contradicts the post text's claim of the event being a 'Newark Return' (implying a return to a previous location or event) and the post text's mention of UFC 328 being a 'breakout network showing'.

retrieved_evidence.text: Commentators and fans described the #UFC328 lineup as an action-heavy card, according to a range of online reactions that appeared in the hours after the final bell. Users on major platforms repeatedly highlighted the pace of the bouts, the frequency of stoppages and the perceived entertainment value across the main broadcast. Posts characterizing the event as one of the more energetic shows in recent months were shared widely, suggesting that the matchmaking and fight outcomes generated sustained discussion well into the following day.

In recent reports about #UFC328, attention has focused on the intensity of the bouts contested inside the UFC Octagon, where fighters exchanged strikes, takedowns and submission attempts over a fast-moving card. According to commentators and fans posting live reactions, the combination of multiple finishes and a high pace throughout the main card contributed to a sense that the event was one of the more energetic UFC offerings of the year, with the in-cage action seen as a key driver of the wider buzz and conversation around the promotion that night.
Asked Google Gemini about the ratings for #UFC328. It provided some very impressive numbers, complete with links to credible sources. When none of the links worked, I followed up:

The mixed martial arts community focused its attention in recent days on an event promoted under the keyword #UFC328, which was highlighted extensively across social platforms. According to posts from the organization’s official account, the card was promoted as a wild night of fights and emphatic finishes, with short clips and images emphasizing late stoppages and dramatic momentum swings. Observers noted that the promotional messaging framed the show as a standout installment in the ongoing series, aiming to sustain fan interest well beyond the live broadcast window.

Wild night of fights and finishes 😤

Who impressed most at #UFC328?

Around the time of the #UFC328 card, there was visible social media activity from both fans and personalities close to the sport, according to online monitoring. Comment threads, reposts and short video reactions circulated widely, with some posts drawing attention to regional identities and athlete backgrounds. Among them, fighter and media figure Natalie Loureda was noted referencing Joshua Van’s national colors, a detail that contributed to broader conversations about representation and fan allegiance surrounding the event.

The conversation around #UFC328 did not stop at highlight clips and individual performances, as online users also engaged in broader discussion of the show’s reach, according to social media postings surveyed in recent days. References to viewership, rankings and broadcast strength appeared as part of the evening’s narrative, typically presented in brief comments or shared images rather than in detailed analyses. In this context, debates over ratings functioned more as an element of fan discourse than as a fully documented examination of the program’s performance.

Natalie Loureda, known for her background in combat sports and her prominent online following, was also referenced in coverage of the #UFC328 weekend, including mentions of her wearing a bright green outfit in promotional and social settings. According to observers, posts linking her public persona with country-themed colors associated with fighter Joshua Van reflected how personality-driven content and fighter-adjacent branding contribute to the event’s ongoing presence across social feeds, extending conversation about the UFC card beyond the bouts themselves and into lifestyle and fan culture narratives.

As reaction to #UFC328 spread across social networks, some posts also circulated figures and graphics related to audience numbers, according to early online observations. These comments often framed the telecast as a strong CBS broadcast, with users sharing snapshots of ratings claims and comparisons to other sports programming. While the exact data behind these depictions was not always specified, the material contributed to a perception among part of the online audience that the event had performed solidly in terms of viewership.

Alongside discussion of specific knockouts and submissions at #UFC328, posts circulating on social media highlighted alleged CBS audience figures that were presented in the form of compact ratings summaries. These ratings-style cards, which emphasized overall viewership and demographic performance, prompted debate among fans and media observers about whether the event had achieved a breakout in mainstream television reach. According to sports business commentators, such early ratings chatter often helps shape perceptions of a UFC card’s commercial impact beyond the core mixed martial arts audience.
My outfit low-key matches the flag of Joshua Van’s country💚🙏🙏 #ufc328

A data infographic detailing the ratings for UFC 328. It features a large header, a highlighted box with average viewership numbers (3.1 million average, 4.2 million peak), and bullet points on demographic performance and comparisons to previous broadcasts.

UFC 328 OVERNIGHT AUDIENCE CHATTER Reports on the ratings for UFC 328, which aired on CBS on Saturday, May 9, 2026, indicate that the event was a significant viewership success for both the network and TKO Group Holdings. According to preliminary data and industry analysis: Average Viewership: The broadcast averaged 3.1 million viewers, peaking at 4.2 million during the main event title fight. Demographic Performance: The event dominated the Saturday night primetime slot in the key MEN 18-49 and MEN 18-34 demographics, marking CBS's best performance in those categories for a Saturday night in the 2025-2026 television season. Comparison: This event outperformed the last major network broadcast (UFC on ABC in late 2025) by approximately 15%, benefiting from the strong lead-in of regional sports programming and a high-stakes championship headliner. UFC 328 CBS Ratings: Strong Numbers for Newark Return (MMA Fighting). TV Ratings: UFC 328 Delivers for CBS on Saturday Night (Programming Insider). Viewership Data: UFC 328 Main Event Peaks Over 4 Million (Sports Business Journal).

text_analysis.alignment_reasoning: The post accurately reports the event details and general sentiment but exaggerates the certainty of the ratings data by presenting unverified screenshots as definitive proof of a 'strong CBS audience number'.
text_analysis.t2_evidence: The post states 'Early screenshots shared after the event pointed to a strong CBS audience number' as a confirmed fact, whereas evidence [7] clarifies these were 'snapshots of ratings claims' and 'the exact data behind these depictions was not always specified', indicating the post treats unverified speculation as established fact.
t2_problematic_subclaims: UFC 328 as a breakout network showing for the promotion
text_analysis.global_assessment: The post is largely accurate regarding the event's chaotic nature and social reaction but overstates the reliability of the reported ratings figures by framing unverified screenshots as definitive proof.

judge.level3_rationale: 尽管比赛场面确实热烈且粉丝关注度高，但社交媒体上流传的收视率图表被证实为不实传闻（alleged），且图片内容经过合成与编辑处理，导致关于该场赛事收视数据的整体结论失真。
judge.reasoning_trace: Textual evidence supports the general sentiment and specific details about Natalie Loureda, but the specific fight results and definitive ratings claims are unverified or speculative. Visuals are authentic. The distortion lies in the certainty of the unverified claims.
judge.core_claim: UFC 328 was a chaotic, action-packed event with strong ratings and significant online buzz, featuring specific fight results and a polished ratings graphic circulating in fight circles.
key_subclaims: UFC 328 was a chaotic, action-packed card that kept fans locked in.
Sean Strickland edged Khamzat Chimaev for the middleweight title.
Joshua Van stopped Tatsuro Taira in the fifth round of the co-main event.
Natalie Loureda referenced Van's country colors.
The event had strong CBS audience numbers and was a breakout network showing.
A polished ratings graphic was circulating in fight circles.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: UFC 328 was a chaotic, action-packed card that kept fans locked in.
subclaim_findings.note: Evidence confirms the event was described as action-heavy and energetic.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: Sean Strickland edged Khamzat Chimaev for the middleweight title.
subclaim_findings.note: No evidence provided to confirm this specific fight result.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: Joshua Van stopped Tatsuro Taira in the fifth round of the co-main event.
subclaim_findings.note: No evidence provided to confirm this specific fight result.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: Natalie Loureda referenced Van's country colors.
subclaim_findings.note: Evidence confirms Natalie Loureda was noted referencing Joshua Van's national colors.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The event had strong CBS audience numbers and was a breakout network showing.
subclaim_findings.note: Evidence mentions users sharing snapshots of ratings claims and framing it as a strong broadcast, but does not confirm the specific 'breakout' status or definitive numbers.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: A polished ratings graphic was circulating in fight circles.
subclaim_findings.note: Evidence confirms posts circulating ratings-style cards and summaries.

judge.verdict_reasoning: The post's core claim about the event's energy and buzz is supported, but it presents unverified specific fight results (Strickland vs. Chimaev, Van vs. Taira) as facts without evidence. Additionally, it frames the circulation of ratings screenshots as definitive proof of a 'breakout' status, which the evidence only supports as fan speculation. The image analysis confirms the visuals are authentic press photos/graphics, so no visual distortion labels apply. The primary issue is the distortion of certainty regarding specific fight outcomes and ratings data.

image_findings.explanation: The image shows a realistic fight scene consistent with a UFC event, matching the context of the post.
The infographic displays viewership data consistent with the post's description of a 'polished ratings graphic'.
The image shows a woman in a green outfit at a promotional event, aligning with the description of Natalie Loureda referencing country colors.
